title: Microsoft Graph API - Auth And REST Conventions

confirmed_facts.fact: Microsoft Graph requests use versioned REST URLs of the form https://graph.microsoft.com/{version}/{resource}, with v1.0 for generally available APIs and beta for preview APIs.
App-only authentication for Microsoft Graph uses the OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow against Microsoft Entra ID and the scope https://graph.microsoft.com/.default.
Microsoft Graph supports OData query parameters such as $select, $filter, $top, and $search, but support varies by resource and endpoint.
Paging commonly uses an @odata.nextLink value returned by the service; clients should follow the nextLink URL rather than constructing the next page request themselves.
When Microsoft Graph throttles a request it returns HTTP 429 Too Many Requests and the Retry-After header should be honored before retrying.

implementation_notes: Default to v1.0 unless the target capability exists only in beta.
Treat nextLink as opaque and replay it exactly for subsequent pages.
Use $select aggressively to reduce payload size and lower the chance of throttling.
caveats: Not every endpoint supports every OData parameter; always verify per-resource docs.
Beta APIs can change without the compatibility guarantees of v1.0.
